context: Checks whether the final artifact follows the liveview-streams instructions from the published Elixir Phoenix Skills plugin.

checklist.name: instruction-1
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Use streams for collections with 100+ items** — regular assigns re-render the entire list on every change

checklist.name: instruction-2
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Use `stream_insert/3` and `stream_delete/3`** for incremental updates — never replace the entire stream

checklist.name: instruction-3
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Use `phx-update="stream"` in templates** — required for stream DOM patching

checklist.name: instruction-4
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Use `stream_configure/3` for custom DOM IDs** — when default IDs don't match your needs

checklist.name: instruction-5
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Verify after adding the template:** Open browser DevTools → Network → WS frames. Confirm incoming frames contain targeted patch operations for individual items, not full list replacements. If you see full re-renders, check:

checklist.name: instruction-6
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Verify after implementing load_more:** Trigger the event and confirm in DevTools WS frames that only the new batch of items is appended, not the entire list. If DOM IDs collide across pages, items will overwrite each other — ensure your ID scheme is globally unique.
